user.title: EV Smart Charger Setup - Step {step}/{total_steps}
user.description: Set a name for this charger entry. The integration exposes the public charging profiles `manual` and `solar_surplus`; Night Smart Charge and Boost Charge are controlled through dedicated helper entities after setup.

data_description.name: Friendly name shown in Home Assistant.

entities.title: Charger Entities - Step {step}/{total_steps}
entities.description: Map the entities that start or stop the charger, set the charging current, and report charger status.

data_description.ev_charger_switch: Switch entity that turns the charger on or off.

sensors.title: Energy Sensors - Step {step}/{total_steps}
sensors.description: Map the sensors used by Solar Surplus, Priority Balancer, and Night Smart Charge. In three-phase mode you map three sensors per quantity (one per phase).

data_description.soc_car: Sensor with the EV state of charge in percent.
data_description.soc_home: Sensor with the home battery state of charge in percent.
data_description.fv_production: Sensor with current PV production in watts.
data_description.home_consumption: Sensor with current household consumption in watts, excluding EV charging when possible.
data_description.grid_import: Sensor with imported grid power in watts. Positive values mean import from the grid.
data_description.fv_production_l2: Solar production on phase L2, in watts.
data_description.fv_production_l3: Solar production on phase L3, in watts.
data_description.home_consumption_l2: Home consumption on phase L2, in watts.
data_description.home_consumption_l3: Home consumption on phase L3, in watts.
data_description.grid_import_l2: Grid import on phase L2, in watts.
data_description.grid_import_l3: Grid import on phase L3, in watts.

hybrid_inverter.title: Hybrid Inverter Mode - Step {step}/{total_steps}
hybrid_inverter.description:
Hybrid Inverter Mode — for zero-export systems with a home battery (Deye, Sunsynk, Solis, Goodwe, Growatt…).

When the battery is full, a hybrid inverter curtails (throttles) solar production to avoid exporting to the grid, so production reads almost equal to consumption and the surplus looks like zero — even though kilowatts of PV are available. Hybrid Mode discovers this hidden headroom by briefly starting the EV charger as a test load and watching the grid: if the inverter ramps PV up in response it 'rides the edge', otherwise it backs off. Worst-case grid cost is bounded (~350 Wh/day).

On battery systems the battery can silently cover the test load (grid stays ≈ 0) and hide whether PV was really unlocked. Map the optional battery-power sensor below so the probe can tell battery drain apart from real solar headroom.

Fine-tune the numeric parameters (max battery discharge, probe / import durations, battery-full threshold) later in the dashboard settings panel.

data_description.hybrid_inverter_mode: Turns on the empirical curtailment probe for zero-export hybrid inverters. You can toggle it any time later from the dashboard — this only sets the initial state.
data_description.battery_power: Optional. Signed battery power in watts — NEGATIVE = discharging, POSITIVE = charging. Lets the probe detect when the home battery (not solar) is covering the EV, preventing false 'headroom found' results. If your sensor uses the opposite sign, create a template sensor: {{ - states('sensor.your_battery_power') | float(0) }}.

pv_forecast.title: Solar Forecast - Step {step}/{total_steps}
pv_forecast.description: Optional sensor used by Night Smart Charge to decide whether to charge from home battery or from the grid overnight.

data_description.pv_forecast: Optional forecast sensor in kWh — used by Night Smart Charge to decide whether to charge from home battery or from the grid overnight.
data_description.pv_forecast_tomorrow: Optional. Map a sensor that reports the next-day solar production forecast in kWh (e.g. `sensor.solcast_pv_forecast_forecast_tomorrow`). Surfaces the orange 'Tomorrow Forecast' chip on the auto-dashboard.

notifications.title: Notifications - Step {step}/{total_steps}
notifications.description: Select the mobile notify services that should receive alerts and choose the person entity that represents the car owner.

data_description.car_owner: Person entity used to gate presence-aware notifications.

external_connectors.title: External Connectors - Step {step}/{total_steps}
external_connectors.description: Configure optional integrations used to publish the nightly energy forecast target.

data_description.battery_capacity: Battery capacity used to calculate the energy needed to reach the configured EV target.
data_description.energy_forecast_target: Optional helper entity where the calculated target is written. Supported domains: `number`, `input_number`.

dashboard.title: Ready-to-go Dashboard - Step {step}/{total_steps}
dashboard.description: Auto-generate a Lovelace dashboard preloaded with every helper entity from this integration. The dashboard will appear in the sidebar as 'EV Smart Charger' with a glass-style UI inspired by iOS 18. Zero YAML, zero configuration.

data_description.create_dashboard: When enabled, a dedicated Lovelace panel-mode dashboard is created at /ev-smart-charger and registered as a sidebar entry.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure Phase Mode - Step {step}/{total_steps}
reconfigure.description:
Choose how your electrical system is wired.

**Single-phase** — one phase (230 V, charging up to ~3.7 kW): you map one sensor each for solar production, home consumption and grid import, as before. This is the most common setup.

**Three-phase** — three phases (230 V each, charging ~11–22 kW): you map three sensors each for production, consumption and grid import (one per phase); the integration sums them and balances the charging speed across all three phases. In three-phase every amperage setting means roughly 3× the power of single-phase (e.g. 16 A ≈ 11 kW) and the minimum is ~4.1 kW — make sure your battery inverter can sustain the chosen power.

data_description.phase_mode: Single-phase is the default and matches most homes. Choose three-phase only if your installation is genuinely three-phase.

reconfigure_sensors.title: Reconfigure Energy Sensors - Step {step}/{total_steps}
reconfigure_sensors.description: Update the energy and SOC sensors used by the integration. In three-phase mode you map three sensors per quantity (one per phase).

data_description.soc_car: Sensor with the EV state of charge in percent.
data_description.soc_home: Sensor with the home battery state of charge in percent.
data_description.fv_production: Sensor with current PV production in watts.
data_description.home_consumption: Sensor with current household consumption in watts, excluding EV charging when possible.
data_description.grid_import: Sensor with imported grid power in watts. Positive values mean import from the grid.
data_description.fv_production_l2: Solar production on phase L2, in watts.
data_description.fv_production_l3: Solar production on phase L3, in watts.
data_description.home_consumption_l2: Home consumption on phase L2, in watts.
data_description.home_consumption_l3: Home consumption on phase L3, in watts.
data_description.grid_import_l2: Grid import on phase L2, in watts.
data_description.grid_import_l3: Grid import on phase L3, in watts.

reconfigure_hybrid_inverter.title: Reconfigure Hybrid Inverter Mode - Step {step}/{total_steps}
reconfigure_hybrid_inverter.description: Enable or disable Hybrid Inverter Mode is done from the dashboard; here you only map or clear the optional signed battery-power sensor used to detect battery-discharge masking during the probe.

data_description.battery_power: Optional. Signed battery power in watts — NEGATIVE = discharging, POSITIVE = charging. Lets the probe detect when the home battery (not solar) is covering the EV, preventing false 'headroom found' results. If your sensor uses the opposite sign, create a template sensor: {{ - states('sensor.your_battery_power') | float(0) }}.

reconfigure_pv_forecast.description: Update or clear the optional forecast sensor used by Night Smart Charge.

data_description.pv_forecast: Optional forecast sensor in kWh — used by Night Smart Charge to decide whether to charge from home battery or from the grid overnight.
data_description.pv_forecast_tomorrow: Optional. Map a sensor that reports the next-day solar production forecast in kWh (e.g. `sensor.solcast_pv_forecast_forecast_tomorrow`). Surfaces the orange 'Tomorrow Forecast' chip on the auto-dashboard.

reconfigure_notifications.description: Update mobile notify services and the car owner used for presence-aware notifications.

reconfigure_external_connectors.description: Update the optional nightly energy forecast connectors.

data_description.battery_capacity: Battery capacity used to calculate the energy needed to reach the configured EV target.

reconfigure_dashboard.description: Enable or disable the auto-generated Lovelace dashboard. Disabling removes it from the sidebar; re-enabling recreates it.

reconfigure_entities.description: Update the charger entities for this config entry.

data_description.ev_charger_switch: Switch entity that turns the charger on or off.

phase_mode.title: Phase Mode - Step {step}/{total_steps}
phase_mode.description:
Choose how your electrical system is wired.

**Single-phase** — one phase (230 V, charging up to ~3.7 kW): you map one sensor each for solar production, home consumption and grid import, as before. This is the most common setup.

**Three-phase** — three phases (230 V each, charging ~11–22 kW): you map three sensors each for production, consumption and grid import (one per phase); the integration sums them and balances the charging speed across all three phases. In three-phase every amperage setting means roughly 3× the power of single-phase (e.g. 16 A ≈ 11 kW) and the minimum is ~4.1 kW — make sure your battery inverter can sustain the chosen power.

data_description.phase_mode: Single-phase is the default and matches most homes. Choose three-phase only if your installation is genuinely three-phase.

charger_model.title: Wallbox Model - Step {step}/{total_steps}
charger_model.description:
Choose how your wallbox adjusts the charging current.

**Tuya** — current is set only to fixed values (6, 8, 10, 13, 16, 20, 24, 32 A); to lower it the wallbox is switched off and on (a few seconds' pause). This is the default and works with the most common wallboxes — pick it if unsure.

**Generic** — accepts any whole-ampere value (6, 7, 8, … 32 A) via a `number` entity: finer control and, when decreasing, the current drops live without interrupting the session.

data_description.charger_model: Tuya = fixed steps (default). Generic = 1 A steps for non-Tuya wallboxes that expose the current as a number entity.

reconfigure_charger_model.title: Reconfigure Wallbox Model - Step {step}/{total_steps}
reconfigure_charger_model.description:

**Tuya** — current is set only to fixed values (6, 8, 10, 13, 16, 20, 24, 32 A); to lower it the wallbox is switched off and on (a few seconds' pause). This is the default and works with the most common wallboxes — pick it if unsure.

**Generic** — accepts any whole-ampere value (6, 7, 8, … 32 A) via a `number` entity: finer control and, when decreasing, the current drops live without interrupting the session.

data_description.charger_model: Tuya = fixed steps (default). Generic = 1 A steps for non-Tuya wallboxes that expose the current as a number entity.
